steps.title: Spot the clause and list structure
steps.content:
Before the blank: independent clause stating that the poem contains three signature elements of Gwendolyn Brooks's poetic [word].
After the blank: list items that are noun phrases, not independent clauses.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
Rule: a colon introduces a list or elaboration only after a complete independent clause. What follows here is a list of noun phrases (not an independent clause).

A: "...poetic style. Terse, compressed lines..." — The period creates a fragment beginning with "Terse." Incorrect.

B: "...poetic style; terse, compressed lines..." — A semicolon must join two independent clauses. "Terse, compressed lines" is a noun phrase, not a clause. Incorrect.

C: "...poetic style: terse, compressed lines..." — The colon follows the complete clause "the poem contains three signature elements of Brooks's poetic style" and correctly introduces the three-item list. Correct.

D: "...poetic style, terse, compressed lines..." — A comma alone cannot introduce a list after an independent clause. Incorrect.
